Closes #116

## What changed

- add `acceptance/problem-explanations/recovery.json` binding the #113 terminal observation, PR #115 native merge fact, and hashes of the merged acceptance artifacts
- add a release-surface test that recomputes the saved snapshot hash, merge binding, existing acceptance comparisons, A/B presentation bytes, snapshots, and mutation callbacks
- preserve the existing #113 Records, human responses, and production code unchanged

## Why

PR #115 was merged before a Done Claim was posted. GTP therefore reconstructs #113 as `halt / terminal_violation`; a later Record cannot repair that Issue. This successor records the failure without rewriting it and provides a new Evidence PR whose Done Claim can be posted before human merge.

## Fixed source head

`e27286d30e955b561da210674f9c0db66d843020`

## Checks

- `PYTHONPATH=src PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` — 133 passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- changed files — exactly the 2 paths in the #116 Contract
- production code — unchanged

## Evidence limits

- repository tests recompute the internal consistency of the saved GitHub snapshot; they do not make live API calls
- the base test-file hash is bound to the immutable main commit URL because this successor necessarily changes that same test file
- existing human responses are byte-checked, not reinterpreted
- this PR, CI, Evidence, and Done Claim do not grant merge authority
